title: Son of a Witch
anchor_summary: A co-op-friendly action roguelike dungeon crawler built around procedural runs, class-based heroes, item and weapon synergies, room-clearing combat, and local/online multiplayer.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify the game as an 'action roguelike for 1-4 players' with procedurally generated non-linear dungeons, permadeath, random encounters, many bosses, strong item/weapon synergies, and both solo and up-to-4-player co-op. The core loop is clearly run-based dungeon combat with repeated randomized runs and build variation, which strongly matches co_op_action_roguelite. Combat is dominant and supports melee, magic, and ranged playstyles. Unlockable characters and persistent alternative RPG mode support light metaprogression/buildcraft without changing the main roguelite identity.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: 4-player co-op roguelike with side-view dungeon runs, randomized loot, character builds, and boss-focused progression.
Co-op action roguelite centered on procedural dungeon runs, class selection, loot upgrades, and repeated run-based progression.
Class-based cooperative dungeon crawling with room combat, enemy waves, loot pickup, and distinct melee/ranged roles.
Multiplayer action roguelite with procedural runs, unlockable characters, chaotic item interactions, and high replayability from build variety.
Fast action roguelike focused on randomized runs, spell/item combinations, and skill-driven combat with co-op support.

title: Mordheim: City of the Damned - Witch Hunters
anchor_summary: Additive DLC for Mordheim: City of the Damned that adds a new Witch Hunters warband and campaign, inheriting the base game's turn-based tactical skirmishes, squad building, and persistent unit progression.

similar_games.title: Mordheim: City of the Damned
similar_games.why_similar: This DLC directly inherits the base game's turn-based warband tactics, campaign structure, gear management, and persistent roster progression.

similar_games.title: Necromunda: Underhive Wars
similar_games.why_similar: Another Games Workshop squad tactics game built around skirmish missions, gang customization, and persistent fighter development.

similar_games.title: Battle Brothers
similar_games.why_similar: Focuses on managing a small roster through punishing tactical battles with injuries, equipment choices, and long-term squad growth.

similar_games.title: XCOM 2
similar_games.why_similar: Turn-based squad combat with cover, class builds, permadeath pressure, and campaign-driven soldier progression.

similar_games.title: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate - Daemonhunters
similar_games.why_similar: A Warhammer tactical campaign centered on elite squad deployment, ability synergy, and persistent unit upgrades between missions.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence clearly indicates this is DLC and provides rich lore/unit-loadout text for a new warband, including weapons, perks, spells, and a playable campaign. However, because no likely parent game is supplied, the DLC cannot safely inherit the parent game's core gameplay identity. The DLC text does not directly establish enough of the required gameplay-shape signals on its own: perspective, combat structure, session structure, world topology, and whether it is primarily turn-based tactics vs tactical RPG are all insufficiently grounded. Genre tags of RPG/Strategy and repeated references to tactical acumen are not enough by themselves for a confident V2 archetype assignment.
